behavior_in_task_description.reason: Every assert in the tests checks for identifiers, method signatures, constant names, class names, field types, and patterns that are explicitly named in instruction.md (e.g., sessionReadTimeout, READ_TIMEOUT_BUFFER, NodeClientKey, longFrom, fetchNodeTimeout, nodeTimeoutCache, httpClients map type, compareTo/plus patterns, equals/hashCode, the file path, Bazel build target, etc.).

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md prescribes the exact solution: specific method names (sessionReadTimeout, longFrom, fetchNodeTimeout), exact constant name and value (READ_TIMEOUT_BUFFER = Duration.ofSeconds(30)), exact inner class name (NodeClientKey) with exact fields, exact map type change, and exact computation formula. This is not describing what's broken — it's dictating the implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: exact method signature 'static Duration sessionReadTimeout(Capabilities caps)', exact constant name 'READ_TIMEOUT_BUFFER', exact class name 'NodeClientKey', exact field names 'private final URI uri' and 'private final Duration readTimeout', exact method 'Optional<Duration> fetchNodeTimeout', exact field 'ConcurrentMap<URI, Duration> nodeTimeoutCache', exact map type 'ConcurrentMap<NodeClientKey, CacheEntry> httpClients'. An alternative correc

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All 8 fail-to-pass tests exclusively use string/regex matching on file contents (read_file + assert 'X' in content / re.search). None import, instantiate, or call any Java code to verify behavior. The pass-to-pass tests do run bazel build/test, but the f2p tests are purely textual.

test_not_tautological.reason: All f2p tests check for string presence in the source file. A developer could paste any code containing the required string literals (e.g., dead code comments or unused variables with the right names) and pass all f2p tests without implementing any actual timeout logic.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All requirements listed in instruction.md (sessionReadTimeout, longFrom, READ_TIMEOUT_BUFFER, fetchNodeTimeout, nodeTimeoutCache, effective timeout calculation, NodeClientKey, httpClients map type change) have corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly grouped with section comments (fail-to-pass vs pass-to-pass), each test has a descriptive name and docstring explaining what it checks and why.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task doesn't produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB schemas.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, variable names, or method signatures across instruction.md and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names the exact file to modify (HandleSession.java), exact method names to add, exact constant names and values, exact inner class structure, and exact field types — far beyond what's needed for a bug description.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config-edit task.
